desc: Parameters g, h, i, and j are accepted but never used, obscuring the function contract and allowing caller mistakes to go unnoticed.
When d is zero, doSomething returns the unexplained value 999999 instead of explicitly reporting or representing the invalid division.
doSomething uses deeply nested conditionals with unrelated inputs and outcomes, making its behavior difficult to understand, test, and safely modify.
Numerous unexplained constants and sentinel results such as 1234, 42, 123456789, and -1 make the business rules and return-value semantics unclear.
processData implicitly depends on the mutable global dataList instead of accepting its input explicitly, reducing testability and making results sensitive to external mutation.
